summary: Iteration 2: Cookie-based auth migration testing complete. All 15 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests verified login, logout, session persistence, and localStorage removal. Fixed critical bug in FastAPI proxy that was caching cookies between users.

integration_issues.issue: Sidebar collapsed sections may not persist after reload - needs verification. The preferences API is called but UI state may not be rehydrating correctly.

action_items: Verify sidebar collapsed sections persistence is working correctly (may be a timing issue in test)
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: FastAPI proxy was using shared httpx.AsyncClient that cached cookies between users - security vulnerability. Now creates fresh client per request.
Cookie auth migration is complete - no localStorage usage found in /app/src
HttpOnly cookie with SameSite=Lax is correctly set on login
Backward compatibility with Bearer token is working
RLS isolation works correctly with cookie-based auth

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all core flows working, minor sidebar persistence issue)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Cookie-based auth migration is complete. All backend APIs work with HttpOnly cookies. localStorage is no longer used. The FastAPI proxy at /app/backend/server.py was fixed to not cache cookies between users. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_cookie_auth_migration.py covers all cookie auth scenarios.

frontend: Login page loads with empty localStorage - PASSED
Login sets HttpOnly cookie, no token in localStorage - PASSED
Session persists after page reload via cookie - PASSED
Logout clears cookie and redirects to login - PASSED
Super admin sees company selector - PASSED
localStorage remains empty throughout session - PASSED

bug_fix_applied.issue: FastAPI proxy was using shared httpx.AsyncClient that stored cookies from Set-Cookie responses, causing cookie leakage between different users' requests
bug_fix_applied.fix: Changed to create a fresh httpx.AsyncClient for each request using 'async with' context manager
bug_fix_applied.impact: CRITICAL security fix - prevented one user's session from being used by another user
